description_long: This is a vintage pipe wrench with a robust, dark metallic head and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism, with visible gear teeth and a knurled adjustment nut for tightening or loosening the grip. The metal components show signs of oxidation and wear, giving the tool a rugged, well-used appearance. The wooden handle is stained and marked, with a slightly bulbous end for improved grip and control. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for heavy-duty tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings. The tool's utilitarian design and visible aging indicate it has seen extensive use over time.
description: A vintage pipe wrench with a worn metal head and a stained wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use. The adjustable jaw and knurled nut are prominent features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded end of the wooden handle and the face of the wrench's head, with the adjustment nut partially visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the wrench, the straight wooden handle, and the detailed jaw mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the back of the wrench head, with the adjustment nut and gear teeth visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the handle and the other side of the jaw, with the adjustment mechanism and wear marks clearly seen.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a slightly bulbous end, stained and visibly worn, providing a basic grip without ergonomic enhancements
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head with a flat striking face, gear teeth, and a knurled adjustment nut; not a traditional hammer head
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible scratches, stains, oxidation, and wear on both the metal head and wooden handle
